metadata.source: DeFOSPAM openrequirements-results.json (Royal Navy USV Perception T&E)

g_goals.feature: Automated requirements decomposition to test conditions
g_goals.business_goal: Reduce requirements analysis effort by 3-5× and establish traceable ODD-to-test-condition mapping
g_goals.value_statement: T&E Engineers can decompose ODD, safety and mission requirements into testable atoms automatically, enabling faster Phase 1 qualification and reducing manual analysis bottlenecks
g_goals.success_metric: Requirements decomposition completes in <2 engineer-days per system release (baseline: 5-10 days); traceability matrix achieves 100% ODD-to-atom mapping with zero gaps
in_scope: Automated decomposition of ODD environment variable vectors
Extraction of testable condition atoms
Traceability matrix generation with requirement ID linking
Gap detection in underspecified ODDs
out_of_scope: Safety-critical decision making without human review

g_goals.feature: Sensor log ingestion and real-world data curation
g_goals.business_goal: Establish authoritative, classified-capable real-world baseline for model evaluation
g_goals.value_statement: Data Engineers can ingest, curate and index real sensor logs from AUVLAB and MOD sea-trial archives, ensuring model evaluation rests on authentic maritime data without manual catalogue work
g_goals.success_metric: >500 hours of labelled real sensor logs ingested and catalogued per PSRQ cycle; data provenance and classification metadata 100% auditable
in_scope: AUVLAB radar, visual, IR and LiDAR log ingestion
MOD sea-trial tape parsing and metadata extraction
Classification marking and access-control integration
Data quality assessment and outlier flagging
out_of_scope: Real-time data streaming infrastructure beyond pilot

g_goals.feature: Synthetic sensor data generation for rare and adversarial conditions
g_goals.business_goal: Achieve ≥95% ODD coverage without exhaustive physical trials using physics-plausible synthetic augmentation
g_goals.value_statement: Test Architects can generate 10k synthetic sensor frames from 1k real seeds using StyleGAN and diffusion models, covering rare weather and adversarial scenarios that would be prohibitively expensive to collect physically
g_goals.success_metric: ODD coverage reaches ≥95%; synthetic-to-real gap in model accuracy <3% on held-out validation; all synthetic data traceable to parent frame and transformation log
in_scope: StyleGAN and diffusion-based synthetic image generation
Rare-event augmentation (fog, rain, night, sensor occlusion)
Provenance tracking and versioning for each synthetic frame
Physics validation of inpainted sensor data
out_of_scope: Real-time synthetic data generation during testing
Generative models for new object classes (out-of-distribution generation)

g_goals.business_goal: Quantify model-layer robustness before system-level integration using held-out and synthetic test sets
g_goals.value_statement: Verification Engineers can measure accuracy, calibration and detection confidence across real and synthetic data, establishing measurable baselines for downstream safety case evidence
g_goals.success_metric: Model evaluation report generated per release with accuracy, calibration (ECE), confidence histograms and per-class confusion matrices; <5% accuracy drift between real and synthetic held-out sets
in_scope: Accuracy and F1-score computation on held-out test sets
Calibration (Expected Calibration Error) analysis
Per-class and per-sensor-modality disaggregation
Confidence distribution reporting for safety-case threshold setting
out_of_scope: Active learning loop for data collection

g_goals.feature: Adversarial robustness testing and bounds filing
g_goals.business_goal: Demonstrate certified resilience to adversarial perception failures with filed robustness bounds for every object class
g_goals.value_statement: Safety Engineers can apply IBM ART, Foolbox and CleverHans to generate adversarial examples and file SIL-2-equivalent robustness certificates, enabling AMLAS activity 3.2 sign-off
g_goals.success_metric: Robustness bounds (L-infinity ε, robust accuracy, interaction terms) filed and linked to AMLAS activity 3.2 for ≥95% of declared object classes; bounds reviewed and approved by Safety Case Author
in_scope: Adversarial perturbation generation (L-infinity, L2, L0 norms)
Robustness bound computation per class
Pairwise and compound-condition robustness testing (e.g. fog + patch)
Escalation workflow for SIL requirements breach

g_goals.business_goal: Identify edge cases and brittleness points before deployment through systematic red-teaming
g_goals.value_statement: Threat Modellers can use Garak and PyRIT to systematically probe perception stack failure modes, uncovering latent vulnerabilities and informing ODD domain restrictions
g_goals.success_metric: Red-team report identifies ≥10 credible edge cases per model release; brittleness points logged with remediation or ODD revision; zero high-severity issues slip to Phase 4
in_scope: Systematic adversarial scenario generation using Garak/PyRIT
Edge-case discovery against detection, segmentation and tracking layers
Brittleness reporting and failure-mode documentation
ODD domain restriction recommendations
out_of_scope: Fixing identified vulnerabilities (red-teaming is discovery only)

g_goals.business_goal: Enable audit and regulatory justification of perception-stack decisions to stakeholders and regulators
g_goals.value_statement: Certification Authorities can generate SHAP, LIME and Grad-CAM explanations for model decisions, providing human-readable evidence for Article 36 and AMLAS sign-off
g_goals.success_metric: Explainability report generated per release with SHAP values, LIME explanations, and Grad-CAM heatmaps for ≥50 representative samples per class; regulators approve interpretability narrative
in_scope: SHAP feature-importance computation
LIME local interpretable explanations
Grad-CAM visual saliency maps
Per-sample and per-class aggregated interpretability summaries
out_of_scope: User interface for interactive model exploration

g_goals.business_goal: Validate full decision stack (perception + collision-avoidance + human handover) before sea trials via closed-loop simulation
g_goals.value_statement: System Integrators can run 50+ scripted collision-avoidance scenarios in Isaac Sim, ROS Gazebo and AirSim with physics-realistic sensor feedback, de-risking Phase 5 sea trials
g_goals.success_metric: HIL suite of ≥50 scenarios executes with zero collisions, emergency stops or unexpected human overrides in nominal conditions; degraded-sensor handover triggers correctly in <5 seconds
in_scope: Closed-loop collision-avoidance scenarios (head-on, crossing, overtake)
Human-on-the-loop handover testing under GPS denial and sensor dropout
Telemetry logging and GSN trace export
Sensor-degradation mode simulation
out_of_scope: Hardware integration beyond HIL feedback loop

g_goals.feature: OpenSCENARIO 2.0 and Scenic scenario definition and automation
g_goals.business_goal: Generate 10× more test scenarios per engineer-day via parametric scenario design and combinatorial coverage
g_goals.value_statement: Scenario Architects can define parametric encounters using OpenSCENARIO 2.0 and Scenic DSL, expanding scenario coverage from manual 5-10 per day to 50-100 via pairwise and greedy-maximal algorithms
g_goals.success_metric: Scenario count per release ≥100; pairwise coverage of sea-state × visibility × sensor-mode achieved with <20 core scenarios; each scenario linked to ODD environment-variable vector
in_scope: OpenSCENARIO 2.0 parametric scenario definition
Scenic domain-specific language for encounter geometry
Pairwise and greedy-maximal combinatorial coverage algorithms
Traceability linking scenarios to ODD atoms
out_of_scope: Automatic test-case execution or result analysis
Scenario validation against physics constraints (covered by sim)

g_goals.feature: Targeted physical trial planning and weather-state validation
g_goals.business_goal: Close sim-to-real gap by anchoring synthetic evidence to real weather, comms and littoral traffic
g_goals.value_statement: Sea-Trial Managers can plan targeted physical trials that validate synthetic model generalization, focusing on ODD boundary conditions and weather-state drift rather than exhaustive coverage
g_goals.success_metric: ≥3 targeted sea trials executed; model accuracy on real-world ODD-boundary conditions within 5% of synthetic validation; sim-to-real gap quantified and documented
in_scope: Physical trial planning against ODD boundaries (visibility, sea state, traffic density)
Real-world model validation and accuracy measurement
Weather-state and comms anomaly testing
Sim-to-real gap quantification
out_of_scope: Safety certification of actual vessel operations
Long-duration autonomous deployment (>3 hours per trial)

g_goals.feature: AMLAS-structured evidence bundle and GSN safety case auto-generation
g_goals.business_goal: Reduce safety case drafting from weeks to days via auto-generation of AMLAS-structured evidence bundles and GSN claims
g_goals.value_statement: Safety Case Authors can invoke AdvoCATE, D-Case Editor and LLM-GSN to draft a safety case from assembled evidence (requirements, test data, model cards, robustness bounds, drift logs), achieving 95% goal coverage with minimal manual refinement
g_goals.success_metric: GSN safety case draft generated in <1 engineer-day per system release (baseline: 3-7 days); ≥95% of sub-goals supported by linked evidence; human review and approval within 2 days
in_scope: AMLAS evidence bundle assembly (activities 1–6)
Auto-generation of GSN claim trees with goal-to-evidence linking
Template-based narrative generation for safety arguments
Gap detection and escalation for unsupported claims
out_of_scope: Formal GSN verification or model checking
Regulatory review or sign-off (GSN is draft only)
Domain-specific safety norm integration beyond AMLAS/SACE

g_goals.feature: Article 36 legal review evidence export and compliance filing
g_goals.business_goal: Enable Article 36 legal review of collision-avoidance behaviour via machine-readable, auditable evidence export
g_goals.value_statement: Legal Reviewers can export collision-avoidance evidence bundles (perception robustness, decision-layer logic, fail-safe modes) in machine-readable format, supporting regulatory compliance and operational clearance
g_goals.success_metric: Article 36 evidence bundle exported per release; contains collision-avoidance perception bounds, decision-layer traceability, and fail-safe validation; reviewed and approved by legal/regulatory team
in_scope: Machine-readable evidence export (JSON/XML)
Collision-avoidance decision-layer traceability
Fail-safe mode and handover procedure documentation
Article 36 compliance checklist and filing template

g_goals.feature: Live drift detection and continuous monitoring
g_goals.business_goal: Trigger automatic re-qualification and model updates post-deployment by detecting drift on ≥3 environment variables
g_goals.value_statement: In-Service Monitors can deploy Evidently AI, Alibi Detect and Arize drift monitors on sea-state, visibility and illumination, automatically alerting when ODD boundary conditions are breached and model performance SLO is at risk
g_goals.success_metric: Drift monitor live within 2 weeks post-deployment; detects SLO breach (±10% from baseline) with <5% false-alarm rate; automatic re-qualification checkpoint triggered within 2 hours of SLO-red alert
in_scope: Real-time distribution monitoring on ≥3 ODD variables
Statistical drift detection (Kolmogorov–Smirnov, Wasserstein, PSI)
SLO threshold management and escalation workflows
Automatic model snapshot and re-qualification triggering

g_goals.feature: MLOps lineage and automated evidence store update on model change
g_goals.business_goal: Maintain auditability and support continuous re-qualification by auto-updating evidence store on model lineage changes
g_goals.value_statement: MLOps Engineers can track model lineage (code, data, hyperparameters) with MLflow, DVC and Weights & Biases, and automatically cascade evidence-store updates (AMLAS activities, GSN assumptions) when a new model version is promoted
g_goals.success_metric: Model lineage tracked end-to-end with 100% traceability; evidence store automatically updated within 1 hour of model promotion; no manual evidence-store drift; continuous re-qualification pipeline executes in <6 hours
in_scope: Model artifact versioning and lineage tracking (MLflow/DVC)
Evidence-store dependency graph and trigger rules
Automated evidence re-generation on model change
Continuous re-qualification pipeline orchestration

g_goals.feature: Role-based access control and classified data handling
g_goals.business_goal: Maintain security classification and regulatory compliance (JSP 520) while enabling collaborative T&E workflows
g_goals.value_statement: Information Security Officers can enforce role-based access to classified corpora (AUVLAB, MOD sea-trial data), audit all data access, and integrate with MOD DAIS or Crown Hosting, eliminating manual security-compliance overhead
g_goals.success_metric: RBAC policies deployed for ≥3 classified-data tiers (Secret, Confidential, Unclassified); 100% of data access audited; no security compliance violations; integration with MOD approved hosting completed
g_goals.beneficiary: Information Security Officer, Dstl Autonomy Lab
in_scope: Role-based access control (RBAC) policy enforcement
Data classification metadata and audit logging
Integration with MOD DAIS, AWS TS, Crown Hosting
JSP 520 and DEFSTAN 00-056 compliance checks
out_of_scope: Data encryption at rest or in transit (covered by hosting)
Personnel security vetting or clearance management

g_goals.feature: CI/CD pipeline integration and AMLAS artefact traceability
g_goals.business_goal: Automate evidence generation and maintain auditable chain of custody via CI/CD integration of AMLAS artefact pipeline
g_goals.value_statement: DevOps Engineers can integrate AMLAS artefact generation (test data, model cards, robustness bounds, GSN traces) into CI/CD pipeline, ensuring every requirement traces to evidence and evidence traces to code commit
g_goals.success_metric: CI/CD pipeline generates AMLAS artefacts on every model commit; traceability chain (requirement → test case → artefact → commit hash) 100% auditable; pipeline execution time <30 minutes per commit
in_scope: AMLAS artefact generation automation within CI/CD
Traceability linking: requirement → test case → evidence → commit
Artefact versioning and archival to immutable store
Pipeline monitoring and failure escalation
out_of_scope: Container registry management beyond artefact storage

s_ubiquitous_language.acronym_expansion: Assurance of Machine Learning for use in Autonomous Systems
s_ubiquitous_language.agreed_definition: Six-activity lifecycle-based assurance framework for documenting ML safety evidence and qualifying autonomous systems.

Explicitly declared set of conditions (weather, sensor types, traffic density, geography, speeds, lighting) under which the USV perception stack is qualified to operate safely. Must be finite and measurable.

Formal seven-phase evaluation campaign (requirements decomposition, data curation, model-layer evaluation, system-layer evaluation, physical validation, safety case, in-service monitoring) that a USV perception stack must pass for sea-trial and operational clearance.
Formal graphical notation for documenting safety arguments: claims are supported by evidence and sub-claims form a tree structure. Acceptance deliverable for AMLAS compliance in this programme.

s_ubiquitous_language.acronym_expansion: UK International Humanitarian Law Review Protocol
s_ubiquitous_language.agreed_definition: Mandatory UK Defence legal review ensuring collision-avoidance decision logic complies with international humanitarian law and weapons law. Triggers blocking gate for operational release.

Quantified measure of test case exercise across declared ODD states. Acceptance metric: >= 95% of partitioned ODD space exercised in synthetic + real data combined. Partition method to be defined collaboratively.

Quantified bounds on model degradation under adversarial perturbations. For each object class (vehicle, buoy, etc.) and sensor modality, specify certified ε (L∞ or perceptual metric), attack model, and confidence level. Bounds filed in traceability matrix.

Automated production monitoring of >= 3 declared environment variables (sea state, visibility, illumination, etc.) for statistical divergence from training distribution. Triggers canary rollback or re-qualification workflow.

Explicit enumeration or parameterization of ODD (finite set or continuous ranges) that partitions the problem space into testable, measurable conditions. Input to Phase 1, success criterion for Phase 6.

Measurable parameters (sea state, wind, visibility, illumination, sensor gain/noise, traffic density) that define ODD conditions and are monitored for drift in production. Subset declared in Phase 1, >= 3 selected for post-deployment monitoring.

Artificially generated sensor data (images, LiDAR, radar, IMU) from simulators (Isaac Sim, VBS4, CARLA-maritime) or generative models (StyleGAN, diffusion) augmenting real AUVLAB/MOD logs. Must be traceable to ODD partitions and validated against real distribution.

Low-frequency ODD conditions (e.g., heavy fog, maritime piracy, sensor failure) requiring synthetic augmentation to meet coverage targets. Frequency threshold and augmentation ratio to be defined per feature in Phase 2.

Physics-accurate computational model of USV perception and collision-avoidance stack used for closed-loop scenario testing. Replaces majority of Phase 5 physical trials where synthetic evidence validates through digital twin simulation.
Testing methodology combining simulated environment with real or semi-real perception hardware/firmware. Phase 4 digital-twin scenarios run over HSI hardware under controlled conditions.

Capability to interpret and understand why the perception stack makes classification and collision-avoidance decisions. Required for safety case claims and regulatory approval. Techniques: SHAP, LIME, Grad-CAM.

Structured argument and supporting evidence demonstrating that USV perception is acceptably safe under declared ODD, using GSN notation. Delivered as GSN diagram + traceability matrix + test evidence artefacts. Human-approved by safety engineer before release gate.

Comprehensive AMLAS-compliant deliverable including: safety case (GSN), traceability matrix, test results, adversarial robustness bounds, drift-monitor configuration, Article 36 legal opinion. Versioned in MLOps artefact store.

Bidirectional mapping from requirements (ODD, safety, mission acceptance criteria) to test cases, evidence artefacts, GSN claims, and acceptance sign-off. Maintained in version control. Auditable chain of evidence for certification.

Automated practices for model versioning, training pipeline, testing, deployment, and post-deployment monitoring. Integrates AMLAS artefact pipeline (lineage, evidence store sync on model update). CI/CD gate: all tests pass and evidence bundle approved.

Automated pipeline: code commit triggers model training, evaluation tests, evidence bundle generation, traceability validation, and artefact versioning. Release gate enforces safety-case approval and Article 36 sign-off before deployment.
UK Ministry of Defence scientific research establishment providing autonomy assurance expertise, infrastructure (Autonomy Lab), and classified data access for Royal Navy USV T&E programme.
UK Ministry of Defence organisation for AI capability development and assurance. Co-leads AI Test Assurance Cell with York CfAA and Dstl; provides governance oversight and AMLAS compliance review.

s_ubiquitous_language.acronym_expansion: University of York Centre for Assuring Autonomy
s_ubiquitous_language.agreed_definition: Academic research centre developing AMLAS assurance methodology. Provides framework governance, training, and independent assurance cell participation for Royal Navy programmes.
Structured methodology for building and documenting safety assurance cases. Harmonises with AMLAS six-activity framework; GSN is the notation; this tool is the process framework.

Scenario where USV autonomously detects operational limit (e.g., ODD boundary, high uncertainty) and safely transitions control to remote human operator within defined latency and communication availability.

Operating scenarios where one or more sensor modalities (LiDAR, radar, camera) experience reduced performance (noise increase, occlusion, signal loss). Phase 4 system-layer tests must validate collision-avoidance under declared degraded-mode combinations.

In adversarial robustness context: distinct categories (e.g., merchant vessel, fishing vessel, buoy; OR radar-only, camera-only, fused) for which bounds are computed separately. Matrix: object classes x sensor modalities.

Testing approach that generates test cases from metamorphic relations (e.g., 'if input A produces output X, then rotated input A' should produce deterministic output relative to X). Addresses oracle problem for rare/adversarial scenarios.

Adversarial security testing: use tools (Garak, PyRIT, Foolbox, CleverHans) to systematically probe model vulnerabilities and generate adversarial examples targeting declared ODD boundaries and safety-critical scenarios.

MIT Marine Perception dataset containing real sensor logs (radar, visual, infrared, LiDAR) from surface vessels. Baseline real-world data for Phase 2 curation and Phase 3 held-out test set validation.

s_workshops.feature: Automated requirements decomposition to test conditions
workshop_questions: What is the input to requirements decomposition: user story only, or does it also consume formal ODD specifications, safety requirements, and mission KPIs?
How do we partition ODD into discrete/continuous testable conditions? Who decides the partition granularity (coarse, medium, fine)?
What is the traceability matrix entry format and versioning strategy? Which tool owns it (Jira, Git, Doors)?
How do we measure the '3-5× speedup' claim? What is the baseline manual effort, and what are the unit and conditions?
Acceptance gate: what makes Phase 1 output 'approved for Phase 2'? Coverage of all ODD partitions in traceability matrix?
deliverables: ODD partition strategy document (discrete states vs. continuous ranges)
Traceability matrix template and entry examples
Baseline effort measurement for manual vs. AI-assisted decomposition

s_workshops.feature: Sensor log ingestion and real-world data curation
workshop_questions: AUVLAB baseline is fixed; which MOD classified sea-trial tapes are available for Phase 2? Access timeline and data-sharing agreements?
Data curation acceptance criteria: what constitutes 'labelled and usable'? Labelling accuracy threshold? QA process?
How do we handle missing modalities (e.g., no LiDAR in a sea-trial tape)? Exclusion or synthetic fusion?
Privacy/Classification: which records are releasable to AI tools (Labelbox, FiftyOne)? Governance handoff point?
Versioning and traceability: how do we track which raw log version was used in which PSRQ report?
deliverables: Data QA checklist and acceptance criteria
Classification handling procedures for synthetic-data pipeline

s_workshops.feature: Synthetic sensor data generation for rare and adversarial conditions
workshop_questions: Rare-event frequency threshold: is <1% occurrence rare? <5%? Context-specific (UK littoral vs. global)?
Adversarial condition definition: which threat model (physical-world attack, digital spoofing, sensor manipulation)? Article 36 scope?
Synthetic validation: how do we prove synthetic distribution matches real ODD? Statistical distance metric and tolerance?
Simulator fidelity: when is VBS4/CARLA/Isaac sufficient vs. when do we need MOD-specific maritime plugins?
Provenance and reproducibility: trace every synthetic image back to ODD partition, seed, simulator version?
deliverables: Rare-event definition and augmentation ratio policy
Threat model taxonomy (physical, digital, sensor, comms) with scope boundaries

workshop_questions: Accuracy metric: is it F1? Precision? Recall? Per-class or macro-averaged? Decision boundary on training vs. held-out data?
Calibration method: Platt scaling, temperature scaling, Dirichlet? On held-out set only or production monitoring?
Held-out test-set design: stratified by ODD partition? Held-out synthetic vs. real? Size and composition?
Failure modes: what counts as 'accuracy failure'? Threshold confidence drop per class? Acceptance SLA?
Phase 3 to Phase 4 transition: what accuracy level gatesPhase 4 entry? Can we proceed with 85% F1, or is 95% mandatory?
deliverables: Per-class accuracy and calibration acceptance criteria
Held-out test-set composition and stratification strategy
Accuracy gate criteria for Phase 4 entry

s_workshops.feature: Adversarial robustness testing and bounds filing
workshop_questions: Robustness-bound definition: certified ε (L∞, L2, perceptual), empirical worst-case, or confidence interval? Which metric is mandatory for Article 36?
Class matrix: object classes (vessel, buoy, debris) × sensor modalities (radar, camera, LiDAR)? Or environment classes (fog, rain, night)?
Attack surface: which threat models (digital perturbation, physical objects, signal spoofing, comms jamming)? In scope for PSRQ?
Validation method: red-teaming tools (Foolbox, IBM ART) + manual adversarial design? Who designs adversarial scenarios?
Evidence filing: where are bounds stored (code, traceability matrix, published paper)? Version-controlled with model?
Red-teaming scope: is it jailbreak-testing (Garak for LLM subsystems)? Sensor spoofing? Comms-layer attacks? Or perception-only?
Scenario generation: automated tools (Scenic, PyRIT) + human expert design? Division of labour?
Validation of red-teamed scenarios: are they realistic? Can a naval threat actor execute them? Operational relevance filter?
Fail-safe design: if red-teaming discovers a vulnerability, what is the remediation path? Rollback? Training? Redesign?
Coverage: how many red-teamed scenarios must we generate per ODD partition to claim 'adversarially robust'?
deliverables: Adversarial scenario library and generation process

workshop_questions: Interpretability techniques: is SHAP mandatory? Or can we use LIME, Grad-CAM, or attention-map visualization?
Granularity: per-prediction explanation (for safety-critical decisions) or per-model summary? Both?
Acceptance criteria: what makes an explanation 'good enough' for safety case? Expert human validation?
Failure scenarios: when interpretation fails (e.g., SHAP values are noisy), what happens? Escalation rule?
Reporting format: plots in safety case? Interactive dashboard in evidence store? Static reports per model version?
deliverables: Explanation acceptance criteria and human-validation process
Reporting template for safety case integration

workshop_questions: Digital-twin fidelity: how close must the simulated perception+collision-avoidance match real hardware? Physics engine requirements?
Scenario design: how are scenarios generated for Phase 4? From ODD partitions? From rare-event synthesis? From red-teaming?
Closed-loop test acceptance: what constitutes 'collision avoided'? Did the system make a valid decision or just lucky?
Human-on-the-loop scenarios: what latency is acceptable for remote operator handover? What comms bands are in scope?
Degraded-sensor validation: which sensor combinations are tested in Phase 4? All, or sampled by risk priority?
deliverables: Scenario library and acceptance criteria for Phase 4

s_workshops.feature: OpenSCENARIO 2.0 and Scenic scenario definition and automation
workshop_questions: Scenario language choice: OpenSCENARIO 2.0 or Scenic? Mixed? Mapping between them?
Parameterization: are scenarios parameterized by ODD partitions? Can we auto-generate scenario variants from ODD ranges?
Validation: how do we verify that a Scenic/OpenSCENARIO file is executable in our digital twins (Isaac Sim, VBS4, etc.)?
Traceability: how do scenarios link to requirements and test results? Scenario version in evidence bundle?
Reproducibility: seeding and determinism — can we re-run the exact same scenario for regression testing?
deliverables: Scenario language specification and tooling integration

s_workshops.feature: Targeted physical trial planning and weather-state validation
workshop_questions: Phase 5 scope: which synthetic scenarios must be anchored with physical trials? All, or risk-sampled?
Anchor criteria: what tolerance between synthetic and physical results is acceptable? If <5% difference, call it validated?
Trial location and schedule: BUTEC, Portland Harbour, Pentland Firth? Seasonal constraints (weather availability)?
Weather-state coverage: are we sampling UK Met Office archives or running trials across seasons? Cost/time trade-off?
Failure handling: if a physical trial contradicts synthetic results, who decides remediation (redesign, synthetic validation, trial repeat)?
deliverables: Anchor acceptance criteria (synthetic vs. physical tolerance)

s_workshops.feature: AMLAS-structured evidence bundle and GSN safety case auto-generation
workshop_questions: Safety case structure: does it follow AMLAS six activities? Which activity gets mapped to which GSN subtree?
Auto-generation scope: what can be auto-generated from test results? Claim structure? Evidence pointers? Or only narrative skeleton?
Human approval gate: who approves the GSN? T&E Engineer, Safety Engineer, or both? Can they approve incrementally (per phase)?
Completeness check: what makes a GSN 'done'? All leaf claims supported by evidence? Undeveloped claims acceptable?
Version control: is the GSN source (D-Case Editor, AdvoCATE) in Git? Does each model version get its own GSN branch?
deliverables: GSN approval process and sign-off authority

s_workshops.feature: Article 36 legal review evidence export and compliance filing
workshop_questions: Scope: is Article 36 review scoped to perception only, or does it include collision-avoidance decision logic and human-on-the-loop design?
Evidence export format: what does a legal reviewer expect? Safety case? Test results? Threat-model analysis? Failure modes?
Legal criteria: what international humanitarian law principles must the collision-avoidance system satisfy? Proportionality? Distinction?
Rejection scenarios: if legal review rejects the system, what are the remediation options (design change, policy change, coverage gap)?
Timeline: how long does Article 36 review take? Critical path for operational release?
deliverables: Legal-review acceptance criteria and remediation paths

s_workshops.feature: Live drift detection and continuous monitoring
workshop_questions: Drift-monitoring environment variables: which ones (sea state, visibility, sensor temperature)? Who selects them? Risk-driven or exhaustive?
Drift detection tools: Evidently AI + Alibi Detect + Arize? How do they interact? Multi-tool consensus required?
Alert thresholds: what p-value triggers a drift alarm? Per-variable or aggregate? False-positive tolerance?
Canary deployment: if drift is detected, does the system auto-rollback? Who decides on rollback? Escalation path?
Re-qualification trigger: does drift detection directly trigger Phase 1 re-decomposition, or is human review first?
deliverables: Drift-detection threshold and multi-tool decision logic

s_workshops.feature: MLOps lineage and automated evidence store update on model change
workshop_questions: Lineage tracking: does the CI/CD pipeline record model→training-data→hyperparameters→test-results→evidence-bundle links? Format?
Evidence store: is it DVC, MLflow, W&B, or custom? Single source of truth or federated?
Auto-update rules: on model commit, which evidence artefacts are regenerated (test results, bounds, safety-case claims)?
Rollback capability: if a new model fails acceptance tests, can we atomically roll back lineage and revert to prior evidence bundle?
Compliance: is the lineage auditable for GSN evidence chains and Article 36 review? Immutable logs?
deliverables: MLOps lineage architecture and data model

s_workshops.feature: Role-based access control and classified data handling
workshop_questions: Classification levels: which artefacts are classified (MOD sea-trial data, adversarial bounds, threat models)? What about synthetic data derived from them?
Tool restrictions: which AI tools (Labelbox, FiftyOne, W&B) are cleared for classified data? Vendor approval process?
Access control: who needs read/write access to each artefact? T&E Engineer, Developer, Safety Engineer, Legal Reviewer?
Compartmentation: can maritime data be mixed with air-defence data? Or must we segregate by domain?
Cross-domain sharing: for AUKUS collaboration, what can be shared with Australian or US partners? Caveat marking?

s_workshops.feature: CI/CD pipeline integration and AMLAS artefact traceability
workshop_questions: Pipeline trigger: code commit, scheduled nightly, or manual? Different cadences for different PSRQ phases?
Test gate: all tests must pass before release? Or can we promote with warnings and risk acceptance?
Artefact versioning: does each CI/CD run produce a dated, immutable evidence snapshot? Git-tagged model?
Rollback mechanics: if a pipeline run fails, what is the rollback scope (last passing run, last release, manual selection)?
Traceability reporting: does each PSRQ report link to specific pipeline runs? Are pipeline logs part of evidence archive?

automation_layer_design.recommended_framework: Cucumber-JVM (BDD orchestration) + pytest-bdd (ML/signal-processing layer) + Selenium WebDriver (UI steps) + Robot Framework (system integration) + custom Python harnesses for AMLAS artefact validation
automation_layer_design.rationale: Royal Navy USV T&E requires multi-layer automation spanning: (1) Gherkin-driven test orchestration for PSRQ phases, (2) Python-based ML model evaluation with scikit-learn/PyTorch, (3) Robotics-grade HIL simulation harness integration, (4) Evidence bundle generation and traceability matrix validation. Cucumber-JVM provides enterprise-scale BDD platform with strong industry adoption in defence. pytest-bdd handles scientific test design. Robot Framework excels at keyword-driven system-level testing.

layer_1_orchestration.responsibility: Parse and execute 28 Gherkin scenarios across 7 PSRQ phases; maintain traceability tags (@REQ-XXX, @SCEN-YYY, @phase-N); generate test reports
layer_1_orchestration.integration: Jenkins CI/CD pipeline; tags drive phase-gated test execution; test results feed evidence store

layer_2_model_evaluation.responsibility: Model-layer accuracy, calibration (ECE), adversarial robustness (ε-perturbation bounds), drift detection (KS-test); connects to training/validation datasets
layer_2_model_evaluation.integration: Step definitions call scikit-learn, PyTorch, Foolbox, CleverHans; outputs to MLOps artefact store (MLflow/DVC)

layer_3_hil_system.tool: Robot Framework + Python robotics libraries (rclpy for ROS 2)
layer_3_hil_system.responsibility: Hardware-in-the-loop closed-loop scenarios; physics simulator (Isaac Sim, Gazebo) integration; collision-avoidance decision trace logging
layer_3_hil_system.integration: Robot Listener API captures telemetry; scenario parameters driven by Cucumber Scenario Outline Examples

layer_4_evidence_validation.responsibility: Verify evidence bundle completeness (all AMLAS activities 1–6 represented); check traceability matrix for gaps; generate GSN claim templates
layer_4_evidence_validation.integration: Post-execution hook; validates evidence before Phase 6 gate closure

step_definitions.step: Given the USV perception stack is under formal qualification
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Boot PSRQ test harness against candidate model; load ODD specification; initialize evidence store with phase-specific metadata

step_definitions.step: And the PSRQ programme gate for Phase N is active
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Validate that all prior phases (1 to N-1) have completed and evidence is signed off; unlock Phase N test scenarios

step_definitions.step: Given an ODD requirement specifying visibility range X–Y m, sea state Beaufort A–B, and illumination condition Z
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Parse ODD specification from SRS JSON; extract environment variable bounds; initialize traceability atom record with ODD partitions

step_definitions.step: When the T&E Engineer requests automated decomposition to testable conditions
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Invoke decomposition engine (Python function or microservice); apply partitioning algorithm (e.g., uniform bins for visibility, Beaufort scale for sea state); generate requirement atoms

step_definitions.step: Then the process identifies N environment variable vectors
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Count extracted environment variables from ODD spec; assert count == expected; link each to requirement ID

step_definitions.step: And each atom is versioned and linked to the parent ODD requirement ID
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Verify each requirement atom has unique version ID (e.g., ODD-NAV-001-VIS-001-v1); check traceability matrix for parent link

step_definitions.step: Given a curated set of N real naval sensor logs
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Load classified AUVLAB or MOD sea-trial dataset from DVC artefact store; validate sensor metadata (calibration, timestamp, GPS position)

step_definitions.step: When the test architect requests Nx synthetic augmentation for conditions
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Invoke StyleGAN or diffusion-model augmentation pipeline with ODD condition parameters; track parent-to-synthetic frame lineage in metadata log

step_definitions.step: Then generative model produces N synthetic frames with logged provenance
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Count synthetic output frames; validate provenance JSON for each (parent ID, transformation parameters); assert all synthetic frames linked to parent

step_definitions.step: Given a detection model trained on real sensor dataset + synthetic augmentation, evaluated on N-image held-out set
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Load trained model checkpoint from MLflow; prepare hold-out test set (images pre-split, ground-truth annotations loaded); initialize evaluation metrics dict

step_definitions.step: When the verification engineer measures accuracy (mAP), precision, recall and calibration (ECE, MCE)
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Call pycocotools (mAP), sklearn.metrics (precision, recall), custom calibration function (ECE via binning); collect per-class and per-modality breakdowns

step_definitions.step: Then the model achieves X% mAP, Y% precision, Z% recall, ECE=W
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Assert reported metrics within expected ranges; log model card with metrics; check that ECE < 0.1 (well-calibrated threshold); flag overconfidence if ECE > 0.15

step_definitions.step: Given a collision-avoidance stack running in HIL with physics-realistic sensor feedback
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Initialize physics simulator (Isaac Sim, Gazebo, or AirSim); boot collision-avoidance ROS 2 nodes; synchronize sensor feedback loop (10 Hz nominal)

step_definitions.step: When the system integrator executes N scripted encounter scenarios (head-on, crossing, overtake)
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Load scenario script (OpenSCENARIO format or custom JSON); inject vessels at specified ranges/bearings; run closed-loop simulation; log helm commands and collision events

step_definitions.step: Then all N scenarios complete without collision, emergency stop or human override
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Assert zero collision detection events (distance < 50m); assert zero emergency-stop triggers; assert zero human-override requests; log scenario completion status

step_definitions.step: And telemetry is logged and traces are exported to the GSN safety case
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Save sensor telemetry (radar, camera, fusion outputs) to HDF5; export decision trace (perception confidence → collision avoidance decision → helm command) to JSON; link to GSN claim ID

step_definitions.step: Given HIL scenario with GPS denial and Y sensor loss, forcing reliance on Z sensors
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Configure physics simulator to disable GPS and specified sensor; verify fallback sensors remain active; initialize confidence thresholds for multi-sensor fusion

step_definitions.step: When the USV autonomy stack detects confidence falling below handover threshold (70%)
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Monitor fusion-layer confidence metric in real-time; trigger handover logic when confidence < 70%; measure elapsed time to handover alert

step_definitions.step: Then a safe-state transition fires: the system reduces speed to 2 knots and raises human-control alert
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Assert helm-command speed <= 2 knots; assert human-alert flag set to true; log timestamp of transition; record alert acknowledge latency

step_definitions.step: And the human operator assumes manual helm within N seconds; event logged as successful handover
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Simulate human-in-the-loop by injecting manual helm command after <N seconds; assert helm ownership transferred; log handover success status

step_definitions.step: Given a scenario DSL script defining encounter geometry with N factors
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Parse OpenSCENARIO 2.0 or Scenic script; extract factor names and levels (e.g., {vessel-distance: [3, 5, 10] nm, approach-angle: [30, 60, 90, 120] deg}); initialize combinatorial coverage matrix

step_definitions.step: When the scenario architect invokes pairwise (2-way) coverage generation
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Call pairwise coverage algorithm (e.g., PICT or custom greedy) on factors; output minimal scenario set; validate coverage > 95%

step_definitions.step: Then the framework generates M minimal encounter variants covering all pairs
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Assert scenario count <= theoretical minimum for pairwise coverage; verify each pair of factors represented; log uncovered pairs (if any)

step_definitions.step: Given auto-generated GSN claim lacking system-layer context
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Load draft GSN claim from auto-generation tool; inspect claim text and supporting evidence links; flag if no HIL scenario reference found

step_definitions.step: When the safety case author (human) reviews the claim and finds no link to system-layer HIL outcomes
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Human reviewer manually inspects claim; searches evidence store for matching HIL scenario trace; flags claim as requiring refinement

step_definitions.step: Then the human rejects the claim and requests manual refinement
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Record rejection in evidence-bundle audit log; mark claim status as REJECTED; queue for human re-authoring

step_definitions.step: Given complete AMLAS activity evidence
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Validate presence of all 6 AMLAS activity artefacts (requirements, test data, model development record, model verification report, operational monitoring procedures, re-qualification trigger definition); aggregate into evidence bundle JSON

step_definitions.step: When the safety case author invokes auto-generation with evidence management tool and safety case generation tool
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Call evidence-bundling service (e.g., AdvoCATE, LLM-GSN wrapper); supply AMLAS activity artefacts; request GSN tree generation with goal-to-evidence mapping

step_definitions.step: Then a draft GSN tree is generated with top-level safety goal linked to 6 AMLAS activities
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Verify GSN tree structure (root goal → 6 sub-goals, one per activity); check that each sub-goal references ≥1 evidence artefact; validate graph is acyclic and well-formed

step_definitions.step: Given live deployment with statistical drift monitoring on environment variable X
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Initialize drift-detection microservice (Evidently AI, Alibi Detect, or custom); configure statistical test (KS, Wasserstein) and baseline distribution from training data; set alert thresholds (SLO±10%)

step_definitions.step: When observed sea state drifts from training envelope to measured Beaufort N for >1 hour
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Simulate live sensor stream producing Beaufort N readings for >60 minutes; accumulate samples; compute distribution divergence statistic

step_definitions.step: Then the drift detector computes P-value < 0.05 (Kolmogorov–Smirnov test vs. training distribution)
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Execute KS test on observed vs. baseline; assert p-value < 0.05; record test statistic and threshold used

step_definitions.step: And an automatic alert is issued: Re-qualification required; model performance SLO at risk
step_definitions.implementation_hint: Assert SLO-red alert flag set; log alert timestamp, environment readings, statistical evidence; trigger model snapshot and re-qualification pipeline

shared_steps: Given the USV perception stack is under formal qualification
And the PSRQ programme gate for Phase N is active
Given an ODD requirement specifying visibility range X–Y m, sea state Beaufort A–B
When the test architect requests Nx synthetic augmentation

a_automation.framework_rationale: The USV perception T&E programme spans 7 formal phases (requirements → data → model → system → real-world → safety case → in-service), each with distinct test concerns: Phase 1–2 focus on requirements traceability and data provenance (Gherkin + Python data-validation steps); Phase 3 emphasizes ML model metrics and robustness bounds (pytest-bdd + scikit-learn/Foolbox); Phase 4–5 demand closed-loop system integration (Robot Framework + Isaac Sim); Phase 6 requires evidence bundling and GSN reasoning (custom AMLAS validator); Phase 7 enforces real-time drift monitoring (statistical harness). Cucumber-JVM orchestrates all phases via unified tag-driven execution model, enabling auditable phase gates and traceability matrix integration critical for AMLAS compliance and Article 36 regulatory sign-off.

ci_cd_integration.trigger: Model code commit to main branch
pipeline_steps: Stage 1: Run Cucumber Phase 1–2 tests (requirements decomposition + data validation); gate on 100% traceability
Stage 2: Run pytest-bdd Phase 3 model evaluation (accuracy, calibration, adversarial bounds); gate on mAP ≥ baseline
Stage 3: Run Robot Framework Phase 4 HIL scenarios (50 collision-avoidance tests); gate on zero safety-critical failures
Stage 4: Invoke Phase 5 real-world trial comparison (if available); flag sim-to-real gap > 5%
Stage 5: Generate Phase 6 AMLAS evidence bundle and draft GSN; invoke human safety review gate
Stage 6: Update Phase 7 in-service monitoring config with new model snapshot; validate drift-monitor integration
Final: Tag model artefact with passing pipeline run; release to operational service
gate_criteria: All Gherkin scenarios passing with @critical tags
Model accuracy within 3% of baseline on held-out test set
GSN draft safety case with ≥95% goal-evidence coverage
Article 36 evidence bundle signed by Legal Reviewer

validation_strategy.context: 84 DeFOSPAM findings (31 critical, 50 major, 3 minor) across 16 features mapped to 28 refined scenarios + 40 examples; AMLAS-gated CI/CD with Dstl HAWC classified compute

smoke.purpose: Critical model-layer accuracy sanity, ODD gate, drift-monitor heartbeat
smoke.rationale: DeFOSPAM critical findings (31) map to Phase 1-3 activities. Smoke selects 12 scenarios covering: (1) ODD decomposition + traceability (SCEN-001,002 from REQ-001), (2) model accuracy baseline (SCEN-021 mAP gate), (3) drift detection infrastructure (SCEN-013 SLO alert), (4) critical adversarial robustness (SCEN-007 bounds filing). Examples EX-002 (100% traceability happy path), EX-014 (accuracy baseline), EX-022 (calibration check), EX-034/037/040 (drift sanity, GSN generation sanity) ensure rapid feedback. All fit within 5 min by executing only model-layer assertions + simulator initialization checks, no HIL.

regression.purpose: Phase 3-4 model robustness, system-layer HIL basics, scenario coverage
regression.rationale: DeFOSPAM major findings (50) map to Phase 3-4 qualification gates. Regression selects 18 scenarios covering: (1) synthetic data provenance (SCEN-004,005,006 with error cases EX-005 for broken lineage), (2) adversarial robustness bounds (SCEN-008,009 with compound-condition testing), (3) system-layer collision-avoidance HIL (SCEN-010,012 baseline encounter scenarios), (4) scenario generation pairwise coverage (SCEN-018,019,020), (5) explainability enforcement (SCEN-024,025). Examples EX-003 (ODD gap detection error), EX-009 (robust accuracy threshold breach), EX-015 (HIL safe-state transition), EX-026 (sensor log ingestion sanity), EX-032 (red-teaming brittleness discovery), EX-039 (Article 36 compliance logging) target major-severity gates. Execution time ~25-30 min via parallel model + abbreviated HIL scenarios (5-10 key encounters per scenario).

full.purpose: Comprehensive Phase 4-7: real-world validation, adversarial robustness bounds, drift-monitor live validation, evidence store freshness
full.rationale: Full suite executes remaining 38 scenarios (all 28 refined + 10 curated examples) covering Phase 4-7 lifecycle gates. Includes: (1) degraded-mode HIL handover (SCEN-011,012 GPS denial + sensor dropout), (2) drift-monitor live deployment & SLO escalation (SCEN-014,015 continuous monitoring + human-override logging), (3) GSN safety case auto-generation & human review (SCEN-016,017 AMLAS artefact linking), (4) scenario DSL combinatorial expansion (SCEN-020 greedy-maximal truncation), (5) sim-to-real mismatch detection (SCEN-022 UK coastal trial accuracy drop), (6) model confidence miscalibration under adversarial perturbation (SCEN-023), (7) red-teaming & LLM security (SCEN-027,028), (8) explainability + sensor log curation (SCEN-026 MOD DAIS lineage). Examples span 30 scenarios validating edge cases (EX-001 boundary partitioning, EX-006 Doppler inpainting physics), error modes (EX-005 provenance rejection, EX-008 small-boat robustness SIL breach), and compliance workflows (EX-031 Article 36 evidence export, EX-035 LLM input sanitization). Execution time ~100-120 min via full HIL scenario suite (50+ collision-avoidance encounters), adversarial bounds sweep (all object classes), real-world validation data ingestion, and evidence store freshness checks.

gates.blocks_explanation: Smoke failures indicate broken ODD decomposition, model accuracy collapse, or drift-monitor initialization failure; must fix before merge to prevent downstream integration failures
gates.remediation: Re-run smoke on latest code; if persistent, revert commit and investigate model drift or data pipeline

gates.blocks_explanation: Regression failures indicate robustness gap (adversarial bounds missed), HIL scenario regression, or scenario generation logic breakage; blocks release to prevent qualified model being overridden by broken version
gates.remediation: Investigate root cause (robustness gap, HIL state corruption, synthetic data lineage break); file major safety exception if bounds exceeded; escalate to AMLAS team if traceability broken

gates.blocks_explanation: Full-suite failure in live-drift monitoring, real-world validation, evidence generation, or red-team discovery indicates unresolved safety gap; requires AMLAS team review before operational clearance
gates.remediation: AMLAS team reviews evidence store, sim-to-real gap quantification, drift-monitor alert fidelity, and red-teaming brittleness report; may require ODD tightening, model retraining, or Phase 5 additional sea trials

notification_strategy.smoke_pass: Silent on main (expected); on feature branch, comment on PR with smoke-pass badge
notification_strategy.smoke_fail: Immediate Slack alert to #uav-t&e-critical; email T&E engineer; block merge via CI status check
notification_strategy.regression_pass: Email Safety Engineer + T&E: 'Regression passed: N scenarios, X adversarial bounds verified, Y HIL encounters passed'; attach dashboard link
notification_strategy.regression_fail: Slack #uav-safety + email; create incident issue; attach failure logs (robustness gap detail, HIL telemetry, scenario coverage gap)
notification_strategy.full_pass: Email AMLAS Team + Dstl: 'Full validation passed: M hours runtime, P sim-to-real mismatch <5%, Q red-team brittleness issues'; ready for sign-off
notification_strategy.full_fail: Email AMLAS Team + Dstl + Safety: attach evidence-store gap analysis; schedule review call; log as safety exception requiring compliance decision

metrics: Phase 1 (Requirements): % ODD atoms mapped (target 100%)
Phase 2 (Data): % synthetic data provenance verified (target 100%)
Phase 3 (Model): mAP trend, adversarial bounds coverage by object class (target ≥95% classes with filed bounds)
Phase 4 (System): HIL scenario pass rate (target 100%), human-handover latency (target <5s)
Phase 5 (Real-world): sim-to-real accuracy gap (target <5% on held-out UK trial), sea-trial weather-state drift (target SLO-yellow <1% false-alarm rate)
Phase 6 (Safety Case): AMLAS activity evidence completeness (target 95% auto-linked), GSN claim rejection rate (target <5% requiring manual rework)
Phase 7 (In-Service): drift-monitor live uptime (target 99.9%), SLO-red alert false-alarm rate (target <5%), re-qualification checkpoint triggered correctly (target 100% of SLO-red events)
dashboard_sections.color_scheme: Red = gate-blocking finding (smoke: regression, regression: major robustness gap or HIL failure, full: drift-monitor unavailable or red-team 0-day); Yellow = major finding (score <85%); Green = pass

metrics: mAP on held-out test set (baseline + rolling 7-day trend)
Calibration ECE trend (target ECE <0.05 clean, monitor under perturbation)
Per-class accuracy disaggregation by environment variable (visibility, sea-state, illumination)
Confidence distribution histogram (monitor for bimodal collapse under adversarial)
Synthetic vs. real held-out gap (target <3%)
dashboard_sections.alert_threshold: mAP drop >2% or ECE jump >0.1 → fire drift-detector review

metrics: Object classes with filed robustness bounds (target 28/28 classes from detection model)
Bound types filed: L-infinity ε (target ≥95% classes), L2 (target ≥50%), L0 (target ≥30%)
Compound-condition bounds (fog + patch, sea-state + adversarial): target ≥20 pairs tested
SIL 2 threshold compliance: % classes meeting ≥75% robust accuracy (target 100%); escalation list if <75%
dashboard_sections.alert_threshold: Any class drops below SIL 2 threshold → block release, escalate to AMLAS

metrics: ODD environment variable partitions covered (visibility: 50/200/500 m, sea-state: 2/3/4 Beaufort, illumination: daylight/dusk/night/night-IR)
Pairwise scenario coverage: visibility × sea-state × sensor-mode (target ≥90% pairs tested in regression)
Combinatorial explosion risk flagged (scenarios truncated by greedy-maximal algorithm → log uncovered pairs in findings)
Per-ODD-atom test count: identify underexplored atoms requiring scenario expansion
dashboard_sections.alert_threshold: Any environment variable uncovered in smoke → yellow; uncovered in regression → red

metrics: Evidence store completeness: AMLAS activities 1-6 linked to test artefacts (target 100%)
Collision-avoidance evidence bundle generated (target per release): includes perception robustness, decision-layer logic, fail-safe mode validation
Human-override compliance logging: % of SLO-red engineer overrides logged with Article 36 exception tag (target 100%)
Legal-reviewer sign-off status: 'approved', 'pending-review', 'blocked' (target approved before operational clearance)
dashboard_sections.alert_threshold: Article 36 bundle incomplete → blocks AMLAS sign-off; override logging gaps → compliance exception

metrics: Time since last successful evidence regeneration (target <24 hours)
Model version delta between current deployment and qualification evidence (target <3 commits)
Drift-monitor alert staleness: time since last SLO-yellow/red event (target <7 days for active deployment)
Synthetic data provenance chain freshness: % of synthetic frames with valid MLflow/DVC audit trail (target 100% on import)
Evidence store integrity: % of claims with live-linked artifacts vs. stale references (target >95% live)
dashboard_sections.alert_threshold: Evidence gap >24h → trigger re-qualification pipeline; deployment model version >3 commits old → roll back or re-qualify

reliability_risks.scenario: SCEN-010, SCEN-011, SCEN-012 (HIL collision-avoidance with sensor dropout)
reliability_risks.mitigation: Implement deterministic sensor-backend state snapshots before each encounter; mock radar/LiDAR data with fixed random seed; run HIL scenarios with 30-second timeout + auto-replay on timeout; log simulator wall-clock time vs. scenario virtual time delta for post-hoc analysis; allocate 10% buffer in regression 30-min SLA (actual target ~27 min execution)

reliability_risks.scenario: SCEN-004, SCEN-005, SCEN-006 (synthetic data generation & provenance)
reliability_risks.mitigation: Set StyleGAN/diffusion seed via numpy + torch.manual_seed(42) before each batch; validate MLflow run ID + DVC hash immediately post-generation with retry logic (max 3 attempts); chunk synthetic generation into 1000-frame batches with independent MLflow child runs; monitor GPU memory pre-flight and fail gracefully if <4GB free; test provenance chain integrity before importing into evidence store

reliability_risks.scenario: SCEN-013, SCEN-014, SCEN-015 (drift-monitor live deployment SLO alerts)
reliability_risks.mitigation: Mock telemetry data source with synthetic sea-state distributions (3-hour baseline sliding window); pre-populate DVC-versioned baseline statistics for each ODD variable; run drift-detector K-S test with min sample size threshold (target n=300) before issuing SLO-yellow to prevent p-value noise; ensure human-override logging uses synchronous writes to classified database with commit confirmation; add 5-minute grace period after drift-monitor initialization before first SLO check to stabilize baseline

reliability_risks.flakiness_probability: high (40-60% validation gap on new trial)
reliability_risks.mitigation: Obtain pre-recorded UK coastal sea-trial telemetry in smoke/regression phases (synthetic proxy using Hensoldt radar simulator + photorealistic rendering); validate against real data post-acquisition; flag mismatch >5% mAP as non-blocking finding requiring ODD tightening or retraining (not a flakiness blocker, but a regression finding); plan Phase 5 sea trials with conservative weather windows (Beaufort 2-3, good visibility) to de-risk novel conditions; maintain separate 'boundary-condition' test set for ODD-edge scenarios

reliability_risks.scenario: SCEN-026 (sensor log ingestion: MOD DAIS classified data)
reliability_risks.flakiness_probability: medium (20-30%) due to data handoff delays
reliability_risks.mitigation: Pre-stage representative AUVLAB curated dataset in Dstl HAWC with full provenance for regression testing; defer full MOD sea-trial corpus to nightly full suite; implement data freshness check (timestamp validation) before ingestion; use deterministic sorting on sensor logs to eliminate ordering variability; validate DVC lineage cross-boundary (UNCLASSIFIED hypothesis tests → CONFIDENTIAL evidence store) with cryptographic hashing; set data-availability SLA at 12 hours pre-run (skip test if data missing rather than fail)

reliability_risks.flakiness_probability: medium (20-40%) due to LLM behavior stochasticity
reliability_risks.mitigation: Fix PyRIT/Garak random seeds and LLM sampling parameters (temperature=0 for deterministic inference); reset perception-stack state between each adversarial scenario (clear model gradient cache, reinitialize batch norm statistics); record seed + model checkpoint hash in each red-team result; run red-teaming scenarios in isolation (separate subprocess) to prevent state leakage; log adversarial perturbation vector + LLM prompt verbatim for reproducibility; accept non-determinism in 'brittleness discovery' phase but require 100% reproducibility in bounds-filing phase

reliability_risks.scenario: SCEN-016, SCEN-017 (AMLAS auto-generation: GSN safety case + human review)
reliability_risks.mitigation: Implement GSN claim validation: every auto-generated sub-goal must link to ≥1 evidence artifact in the store; flag unsupported claims for human review without escalating as failure; use deterministic LLM prompt + context-injection (examples of high-quality GSN claims) to reduce hallucination; timeout human-review feedback at 2 hours (full suite may require AMLAS team async review); log all rejection reasons in findings database; add 'claim-confidence-score' computed from evidence-linkage overlap (target >0.8 before auto-approval)

reliability_risks.scenario: SCEN-023 (model confidence miscalibration under adversarial perturbation)
reliability_risks.mitigation: Run adversarial perturbation generation with fixed seeds (AutoAttack library default seed); validate perturbation magnitude post-generation (l-infinity norm, l2 norm computed deterministically); measure ECE (Expected Calibration Error) on stratified sample of 1000 images with equal class distribution; use multiple calibration metrics (ECE + MCE + Brier score) for robustness; flag calibration jump (ECE delta >0.15) as major finding rather than test failure; implement retry logic for adversarial attack if convergence <90% of target epsilon

summary.defosam_mapping: 31 critical findings → smoke (7 scenarios) + regression (5 scenarios), 50 major findings → regression (13 scenarios) + full (20 scenarios), 3 minor findings → full (3 scenarios)
summary.estimated_execution_time: Smoke: 5 min, Regression: 25-30 min, Full: 100-120 min (sequential) or 60-90 min (parallel HIL + model)
summary.classified_capability: All AMLAS activities (1-6) integrated into smoke/regression gates; classified CI/CD runs on Dstl HAWC + MOD DAIS; evidence store auditable via DVC + MLflow with classification metadata

tooling_recommendation.rationale: MkDocs is lightweight, markdown-native, and easily version-controllable alongside code. Pickles generates living documentation from Gherkin feature files automatically on each commit. Custom HTML publisher for role-based views, ODD heatmap, and scenario search. All static-site friendly for airgapped classified environments (can be hosted on Crown Hosting or MOD DAIS without external dependencies).

cons: Less flexible for classified (Crown Hosting) constraints
alternatives.recommendation: Use if LivingDoc approved for JSP 520 deployment

cons: Not suitable for classified data (UK MOD requirement)
alternatives.recommendation: Not recommended for this classified USV context

cons: Requires GitHub access (not all contractors have it)
alternatives.recommendation: Good for unclassified prototype; escalate to Dstl for classified production

tooling_recommendation.deployment_pipeline: CI/CD trigger (on commit to features/*.feature): Pickles scans feature files → generates HTML documentation → validates Gherkin syntax → checks terminology against chris-language.json → publishes to internal MkDocs site (hosted on Crown Hosting or MOD DAIS) → tags commit with doc version

evolution_process.sync_mechanism: Git-based: developers commit to features/*.feature; CI/CD pipeline triggers on commit, publishes updated docs within 5 minutes
evolution_process.terminology_governance: chris-language.json is source of truth; CI/CD step validates that all refined specs and examples use agreed terms; failures block merge until terminology is corrected or glossary is updated by authorized person
evolution_process.evidence_linkage: Each feature file auto-generates traceability matrix entry linking requirement ID → feature file → refined specs (rex-refined.json) → examples (isabel-examples.json) → test evidence artifacts
evolution_process.review_gate: Feature files must be reviewed by 2 of: T&E lead, Safety engineer, Dstl assurance (depends on phase); merge blocked until approval
evolution_process.versioning: Tag each feature file with PSRQ release cycle (e.g., PSRQ-v2.1); evidence bundle versioning auto-generated on model promotion (MLOps integration)
evolution_process.obsolescence_process: Deprecated features marked with @deprecated tag; CI/CD warns on use; 2-week sunset notice before removal from active docs

l_living_documentation.accessibility_justification: Quick-find capabilities for 'Sea State 5 fog adversarial' scenario: (1) search scenario registry by ODD tags (@sea-state-5, @visibility-low, @adversarial); (2) click AMLAS activity 3 in heatmap; (3) role-filtered index (safety engineer path shows Phase 3 + red-teaming features first); (4) glossary backlinks (search 'fog', see all features mentioning 'rare events' or 'visibility'); (5) Gherkin step index (search 'fog', show all Given/When/Then clauses matching pattern)

consistency_issues.recommendation: Use 'ODD' (preferred) on first mention with expansion in parentheses; chris-language.json defines 'ODD' as agreed acronym
Standardize on 'evidence bundle' where referring to AMLAS-compliant deliverable; chris-language.json defines this precisely
Add 'physical trial' and 'sea trial' as aliases to glossary; recommend 'targeted sea trial' per REQ-010
Clarify: 'declared ODD states' = discrete enumeration/partitions; 'environment variables' = measurable parameters that define ODD. Both are in chris-language.json but examples blur distinction
Always use full form 'AMLAS activity N' on first reference; subsequent uses may abbreviate to 'activity N' with context
chris-language.json uses 'robustness bounds' (plural preferred); 'certificate' is acceptable as sub-artifact but should be clarified in glossary

consistency_issues.location: rex-refined.json REF-027, REF-028; grace-goals.json REQ-006 (related terms used interchangeably)
consistency_issues.recommendation: Clarify: 'red-teaming' is the discipline; 'adversarial scenario generation' is a tool/technique within it. Add to glossary: red-teaming > {adversarial scenario generation, threat modelling}

consistency_issues.location: rex-refined.json REF-024, REF-025 (tool names used, then abstracted)
consistency_issues.recommendation: Per chris-language.json: 'explainability' is capability; 'SHAP, LIME, Grad-CAM' are techniques. REF-024 correctly abstracts to 'explainability technique' in When clause; REF-025 follows. Consistent.
Standardize on 'sensor modality' (chris-language.json uses this for distinct sensor categories: radar-only, visual-only, fused, etc.)

findings.title: ODD partition and coverage metric are undefined — isolation signal from Alexa (ambiguity analyst)
findings.feature: Automated requirements decomposition to test conditions
findings.reasoning: Acceptance criterion 6.1 ('Coverage of declared ODD states ≥95%') cannot be measured without defining what 'declared' means and how coverage is computed. T&E Engineer (business) and Tester have not collaborated to agree on the partition strategy, creating a gating risk for Phase 1 exit.
findings.recommendation: Run three-amigos workshop with T&E Engineer, Data Engineer, and Tester. Agree on ODD partition method (discrete, continuous, mixed), partition examples (3 per method), coverage computation rule, and traceability matrix entry template. Deliver partition strategy document before Phase 1 starts.

findings.title: Adversarial robustness bounds lack type, attack, and class definitions — isolation from Alexa
findings.feature: Adversarial robustness testing and bounds filing
findings.reasoning: Alexa identified that 'bounds', 'class', and 'attack definition' are undefined. Developer, Tester, and Safety Engineer have not agreed on the robustness-bound definition, making Phase 5 acceptance criteria unprovable.
findings.recommendation: Hold all-team workshop (Developer, Safety Engineer, Tester, Threat Modeller) to define robustness-bound type, threat-model enumeration, class matrix (object × sensor × environment), and validation method (red-teaming tools + manual). Deliver robustness specification and class matrix before Phase 3 design.

findings.title: Article 36 scope conflict: perception-only vs. full closed-loop — Alexa ambiguity signal
findings.feature: Article 36 legal review evidence export and compliance filing
findings.reasoning: Alexa highlighted that the document conflates 'Perception Stack' (input) with 'collision-avoidance decision layer' (Phase 4 outcome) without clarity on which is in scope for Article 36. T&E Engineer and Legal Reviewer have not agreed on scope, acceptance criteria, or remediation paths.
findings.recommendation: All-team workshop (T&E Engineer, Safety Engineer, Legal Reviewer, Developer) to define Article 36 scope (perception? planning? both?), success criteria (which IHL principles?), evidence export format, and rejection/remediation paths. Deliver Article 36 specification and legal-review protocol before Phase 6.

findings.title: Missing latency performance non-functional requirement
findings.reasoning: Milarna flagged missing latency bounds for collision-avoidance decision pipeline. DeFOSPAM scenarios define only accuracy metrics; no end-to-end latency SLA (e.g., <200 ms for human handover within 5-second window). Grace's business goals silent on timing constraints. Example EX-029 adds concrete latency measurements (perception 106 ms mean, p99 187 ms) to surface criticality.
findings.recommendation: Add NFR example to EX-029 demonstrating latency measurement at each inference stage (LiDAR ingestion, segmentation, detection, decision) and GPU thermal margin. Define SLA target <200 ms and escalation pathway if p99 exceeds 250 ms. Link to handover-trigger requirement in Phase 4 evaluation (REQ-008).

findings.title: Missing security & compliance non-functional requirement for classified data
findings.feature: Role-based access control and classified data handling
findings.reasoning: DeFOSPAM Phase 3 (Milarna) identified missing security NFR: RBAC policy enforcement, audit logging per JSP 520/DEFSTAN 00-056, integration with MOD DAIS. Grace's goals (REQ-015) specify 'regulatory compliance' but lack concrete examples of access denial, role-based permission boundaries, or audit trail verification. Example EX-030 provides concrete RBAC policy matrix (data_engineer vs. safety_engineer vs. external_contractor) with audit log snippet.
findings.recommendation: Expand EX-030 to include: (1) permission matrix across 3 classified-data tiers (RESTRICTED, CONFIDENTIAL, UNCLASSIFIED), (2) failed access attempt with reason code, (3) quarterly compliance audit checklist per Defence standards, (4) integration test with MOD DAIS or Crown Hosting. Link to evidence store audit trail for traceability.

findings.title: SCEN-028 LLM collision-avoidance vulnerability reveals Article 36 risk
findings.reasoning: SCEN-028: "Garak injects adversarial instructions... model tricked into unsafe behaviour". This is not a unit test failure; it is a weapons-law compliance issue. Article 36 review mandatory. Spec should declare: "Any autonomous decision system using LLM or natural language input shall undergo legal review per Article 36 protocol before operational release."
findings.recommendation: Escalate to requirements: "LLM-based collision-avoidance subsystems are subject to mandatory Article 36 review. Safety case must document that decision logic remains compliant under adversarial prompt injection."

findings.title: GSN claim coverage metric (95% goal-evidence) not automatically validated; Phase 6 gate relies on manual review
findings.reasoning: SCEN-016 expects GSN auto-generation tool to produce 'draft GSN tree with ≥95% goal-evidence coverage,' but current scenario does not verify this metric. Test passes if GSN is generated (regardless of coverage %). In Phase 6, Safety Engineer must manually inspect GSN tree, count total claims, count supported claims, compute coverage ratio, assess whether ≥95% threshold met. Without automated coverage validation, scenarios are not testable: test harness cannot assert 'coverage=95%' because coverage metric is not extracted from GSN output. Furthermore, SCEN-016 variant 'Flag missing evidence for unresolved GSN claims' expects 5% unsupported claims, but does not define acceptance criteria (e.g., which 5%? Why those particular claims?).
findings.recommendation: Implement automated GSN claim-coverage validator: (1) Parse GSN tree (XML/JSON export from auto-generation tool), (2) Traverse graph structure; count total claims (nodes with type='claim'), (3) For each claim, query evidence store for supporting evidence items linked by evidence-node references, (4) Mark claim as 'supported' if ≥1 evidence item found AND evidence artefact type matches claim requirement (e.g., claim about robustness requires evidence_type='robustness_bound'), (5) Compute coverage = (supported_claims / total_claims) × 100%. Create step definition: 'Then the GSN claim coverage is computed as supported_claims/total_claims AND coverage >= 95%' with automatic assertion. For unsupported claims (5% budget), implement secondary step: 'And unsupported claims are: [claim1, claim2, ...] with coverage-gap reasons: [reason1, reason2, ...]'. Integrate validator into evidence-bundling service (Phase 6). Update angie-automation.json to specify GSN coverage validator as part of evidence-validation layer. This enables automated Phase 6 gate closure: gate passes if GSN coverage ≥95% AND unsupported claims < threshold AND Safety Engineer approves unsupported-claims risk.

findings.title: Critical Path: ODD Decomposition Traceability Smoke Gate
findings.reasoning: DeFOSPAM identified 31 critical findings; 12 are Phase 1 requirement decomposition gaps. These two scenarios validate the foundational ODD-to-test-atom pipeline that feeds all downstream phases. Failure here cascades to model evaluation, adversarial bounds, and safety case evidence linking.
findings.recommendation: Prioritize SCEN-001,002 as non-negotiable smoke gates. Add per-requirement traceability report (JSON) to CI/CD dashboard showing atom count and versioning status. Fail merge if any requirement.id lacks linked atoms.

findings.title: Synthetic Data Provenance Integrity: Critical for Evidence Store Audit
findings.reasoning: DeFOSPAM found 15 major findings on synthetic data quality and traceability. Scenario SCEN-005 explicitly tests rejection of provenance-unverified synthetic frames (5000 frames with broken lineage → pipeline rejects with 'synthetic-data-provenance-unverified' error). This is evidence-critical: Dstl HAWC + MOD DAIS classified environment cannot accept synthetic data without complete audit trail. Regulatory (AMLAS activity 3.3) requires 100% traceability.
findings.recommendation: Add synthetic-data provenance audit as a regression gate (sub-30s check). Require MLflow + DVC hashes on every synthetic-frame import. Maintain 'provenance-failure' counter in dashboard; escalate to Safety Engineer if >0 failures in a regression run. Tag SCEN-005 as 'mandatory regression scenario for classified-capability validation'.

findings.title: Adversarial Robustness Bounds Coverage: SIL 2 Threshold Escalation
findings.reasoning: DeFOSPAM identified critical findings on adversarial robustness gaps, especially for small-boat detection (>10 major findings). Regression gate SCEN-008 validates SIL 2 compliance per class. Small-boat class historically achieves only 62% robust accuracy, below 75% threshold. This must be caught in regression to prevent releasing an under-qualified model to Phase 4 system integration.
findings.recommendation: Add 'robustness-bounds-per-class' report to regression dashboard. Plot robust accuracy for each class (radar: merchant vessels, RIBs, small craft, buoys; visual: same classes). Highlight classes <75% in red, 75-85% in yellow, ≥85% in green. Require explicit AMLAS team approval before merging if any class in red. File interaction-term bounds for compound conditions (SCEN-009) in evidence store.

findings.title: Evidence Store Freshness Critical: Model Version Delta SLA
findings.reasoning: DeFOSPAM identified continuous re-qualification (Phase 7, requirement REQ-14) as critical: MLOps pipeline must auto-update evidence store on model promotion. Failure to regenerate evidence within 24 hours means AMLAS compliance chain is broken. Manual deployment with stale evidence = audit violation. SCEN-016 (auto-generation) feeds into this: every model commit should trigger evidence regeneration within 6 hours (continuous re-qualification SLA).
findings.recommendation: Add 'Evidence Store Freshness' widget to main dashboard (not just full suite). Display: time since last evidence regen (red if >24h), model version delta (red if >3 commits), synthetic data audit status (green if 100%), claim-linkage freshness (red if <95%). Integrate with CI/CD: if evidence gap >24h, trigger re-qualification pipeline automatically. Require DevOps to ensure MLflow/DVC evidence update SLA is <6 hours post-merge.

findings.title: Classified Data Handling Rigor: MOD DAIS Integration Critical Gate
findings.reasoning: DeFOSPAM found data curation (Phase 2, requirement REQ-2) as critical for regulatory compliance. Royal Navy USV programme is classified (CONFIDENTIAL). Data must be handled per JSP 520 / DEFSTAN 00-056. SCEN-026 validates end-to-end classified-data pipeline: real sensor dataset ingestion from MOD sea-trial archives, metadata extraction, classification marking, access control via MOD DAIS. Failure here violates security classification.
findings.recommendation: Tag SCEN-026 as 'mandatory for classified-capability release'. Add 'Data Provenance Audit' to full-suite dashboard: display AUVLAB + MOD sea-trial dataset versions, ingestion timestamps, DVC hash chain, classification metadata status. Require Dstl Approver sign-off on data provenance before AMLAS safety-case approval. Include security review as part of full-suite gate (not just functional validation).

findings.title: Article 36 Compliance Logging: Human-Override Audit Trail Non-Optional
findings.reasoning: DeFOSPAM identified Article 36 legal-review (Phase 6, 5 major findings) as critical compliance gap. Article 36 (UK Defence Approval) requires collision-avoidance behaviour to be auditable + defensible. If drift-monitor triggers SLO-red (ODD boundary breach) and human overrides the alert, system MUST log this for post-mission safety review + regulatory reporting. SCEN-015 explicitly tests this workflow. Failure to log override = audit violation.
findings.recommendation: Add 'Article 36 Audit Trail' to full-suite dashboard: display # of SLO-red events, % with logged overrides, override reasons, post-mission review status. Require Legal Reviewer sign-off before operational release: all human overrides must be justified + documented. Integrate Article 36 evidence bundle export (EX-031) into release workflow: bundle must contain all override logs + collision-avoidance perception bounds for regulatory filing.

findings.title: Data lifecycle and evidence retention policy missing — missing perspective gap from Milarna
findings.feature: Synthetic sensor data generation for rare and adversarial conditions
